note: Internal planning data for Build Lab lanes. Track metadata is planning state; artifact inventory must still come from real filesystem sources.

tracks.name: Mission Control shell and module routing
tracks.goal: Ship a usable dashboard shell with Command, Briefing, Build Lab, and Runtime navigation.
tracks.nextAction: Keep the shell stable while runtime adapters and module-level empty/error states are hardened.
tracks.outcomeOrLesson: The shell should stay thin. Runtime and planning data must remain separated at the service layer.

tracks.name: OpenClaw runtime adapter hardening
tracks.goal: Expose real sessions, tasks, cron, model state, and health without inventing interfaces.
tracks.nextAction: Keep using verified CLI-backed sources and expand only when a new runtime source has been confirmed.
tracks.outcomeOrLesson: Sessions, tasks, cron, model config, and health are already queryable. Lobster history is not yet proven beyond the current TaskFlow source.

tracks.name: Bootcamp lesson translation into owned work
tracks.goal: Translate implementation lessons into concrete frontend, backend, and manual task lanes.
tracks.nextAction: Add durable translation notes only when a lesson changes execution or saves future rework.
tracks.outcomeOrLesson: Tutorial input is useful, but it should never be treated as runtime truth or production readiness by default.

tracks.name: Deeper usage cost telemetry
tracks.goal: Show trustworthy request, token, and cost metrics per workload once the runtime source actually produces them.
tracks.nextAction: Revisit after `openclaw gateway usage-cost --json` returns non-zero or more granular workload data.
tracks.outcomeOrLesson: Current usage-cost totals are structurally available but currently zero-valued, so the UI should stay conservative.
